methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
eGenesis, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Luhan Yang and George Church, has raised $250M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Ultima Genomics, Insitro, Colossal Biosciences) and 3 public Biotech & Health comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis using a 1.7x multiplier on the $250M raised-to-date proxy yields $425M. Peer-median anchoring across the three same-stage private comparables—with valuations ranging $1.5B to $4.0B—produces a median implication of $2.4B. Public-comp pricing via P/S multiples (sector median: 5.8x) was not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue.

The triangulated valuation range spans $361M to $2.8B, with a median of $1.4B across both methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method assumes extrapolation from capital deployment rather than operational metrics, while the peer-median incorporates heterogeneous risk profiles across a small sample. Key unknowns include eGenesis's burn rate, clinical-stage risk, and competitive positioning relative to named peers. A narrower range would require disclosed financial milestones or clinical endpoints.
